title: The Last Voyage of the Whydah
subtitle: Plot Black Sam Bellamy's run from the Caribbean to Maine — spring, 1717

wind.note: Light prevailing wind toward the northeast.

currentField.note: Always-on currents. dirDeg = direction the water flows TOWARD (compass). speedKn in knots. The strong NE band is the Gulf Stream; the Caribbean cells feed it through the Windward Passage and Bahamas.

storm.note: Dormant. Activates for the rest of the voyage the moment the track enters the Cape Cod zone — the nor'easter that wrecked the real Whydah. Strong WNW onshore set toward the outer cape.

beats.hatteras: Cape Hatteras lifts to larboard — the Carolinas, and the Gulf Stream at its fiercest. The great river in the sea sets you hard to the northeast; mind your reckoning, or it will carry you further than you know.
beats.block: Off Block Island, Paulsgrave Williams turns the Marianne aside to see his family. 'I'll meet you off Maine, Sam,' he calls across the swell. You sail on, alone.
beats.capecod: Word runs the deck: fresh water and fat prizes lie behind the elbow of Cape Cod. But the sky to the northeast has gone the colour of a bruise.
beats.landfall: Land lifts grey out of the haze off the bow. You have raised the coast at last.

narration.intro: Late February, 1717 — the Windward Passage, between Cuba and Hispaniola. Your crew has just taken a captured slaver: the Whydah, the fastest hull in the Atlantic. Captain Bellamy looks you over and presses the helm into your hands. "You've an eye for the sea. Bring us north." Learn her here, in these calm Caribbean waters — then run her up the coast, past the Bahamas and up the great Gulf Stream, to a safe harbor at Richmond Island, Maine, where Paulsgrave Williams waits. Mind the currents: they set you off your reckoning, just as they did the men who really sailed her.
narration.blockIsland: Off Block Island, Williams turns the Marianne aside to see his family. He raises a hand across the water: 'I'll meet you off Maine, Sam.' You sail on alone.
narration.capeLure: Cape Cod lies off the bow — fresh water, supplies, and word of a fat wine prize ripe for the taking. The richest plunder of the whole voyage, right there. Surely one short stop won't hurt…
narration.plunder: You take the Mary Anne's Madeira and fill the hold; the crew roars its approval. But the sky to the northeast has gone the color of a bruise.
narration.successClean: Richmond Island, off the bow — and your whole crew with you. You did what Black Sam could not: you let the Cape go. Williams waves from the Marianne. Fair winds, Captain.
narration.successStorm: Battered but afloat, the Whydah claws into Richmond Island. You carried plunder through the teeth of the nor'easter and lived where the real Black Sam did not. Fair winds, Captain.
narration.wreck: Breakers roar in the dark. Driven by current and gale, the Whydah strikes the shoals off Wellfleet — just as she did on 26 April 1717. Of 146 souls, a mere two reach shore. Reckon again, Captain: the sea is patient.
